title: The Text-to-Image Challenge
concept: Text-to-image generation must bridge two very different kinds of data — discrete language tokens and continuous pixel grids — into one coherent system.

exercises.prompt: When you read the word 'sunset', what happens in your mind?

options.text: You picture colors and a scene, even though the word itself has no colors
Nothing happens, words and pictures are the same thing
You hear a sound file play automatically

exercises.explain: Right! Your brain turns words (language) into a picture (image) instantly. That jump from words to pictures is exactly what AI image generators have to learn to do too.

cards.title: Two Worlds, One Bridge
cards.body: Type 'a dragon eating spaghetti' and — poof — a picture appears. But words and pictures are TOTALLY different kinds of things. How does an AI turn typed letters into colored pixels? That's the big challenge we're exploring today!

cards.title: What Is the Text-to-Image Challenge?
cards.body: Text is made of discrete tokens — separate, countable units like words or word-pieces. Images are made of continuous pixel grids — smooth grids of color values that blend together. Text-to-image AI must connect these two very different data types into one system so that a sentence can guide the creation of a picture.

cards.title: Sheet Music to Sound
cards.body: Think of text like sheet music — separate notes written on a page, one after another. An image is like the actual sound of music playing — flowing, continuous, all blended together. A musician is the bridge that turns written notes into real sound. AI needs its own 'musician' to turn written words into real pictures.

cards.title: From Prompt to Picture
cards.body: You type: 'a small robot watering a flower on the moon.' The AI breaks this into tokens (small / robot / watering / flower / moon), understands the meaning and relationships, then generates a smooth grid of pixels — shapes, colors, lighting — that matches that meaning. Discrete words become a continuous picture.

cards.title: Myth: The AI Just Searches for a Matching Picture
cards.body: Nope! The AI isn't digging through a folder of existing images to find a match. It's generating a brand new pixel grid from scratch, guided by patterns it learned about how words relate to visual concepts. That's why it can create things that never existed before, like a dragon eating spaghetti.

cards.title: Discrete vs. Continuous Data
cards.body: Text and images store information in very different shapes. Text-to-image models must translate between them.

left.text: Separate tokens, like beads on a string: 'a', 'red', 'cat'

right.text: Smooth grid of pixel color values that blend into shapes

exercises.prompt: Why is text-to-image generation considered a 'challenge'?

options.text: Because it must connect two very different kinds of data: discrete text tokens and continuous pixel grids
Because computers cannot store images at all
Because typing is hard for AI
Because pictures don't have any data in them

exercises.explain: The core challenge is bridging two different data types — discrete language tokens and continuous pixel grids — into one working system.

exercises.prompt: Text is made of continuous data, and images are made of discrete tokens.
exercises.explain: It's the opposite! Text is made of discrete tokens (separate word pieces), while images are continuous pixel grids of blended color.

exercises.sentence: Text-to-image AI must bridge discrete text tokens and ___ pixel grids into one coherent system.
exercises.explain: Continuous pixel grids blend smoothly together, unlike the separate, countable tokens used in text.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes what makes image data 'continuous' compared to text?

options.text: Pixel color values blend smoothly across a grid, unlike separate discrete word tokens
Images have no data structure at all
Images are made of individual letters like text

exercises.explain: Continuous means the values blend smoothly (like colors fading into each other), while text tokens are separate, distinct units.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: 'AI image generators are basically just search engines that find the closest matching photo online.' What's wrong with this statement, and what's really happening?

options.text: Nothing is wrong — that's exactly how it works
The AI actually generates a brand-new continuous pixel grid from scratch, guided by learned connections between discrete text tokens and visual patterns — it doesn't search for existing photos
AI image generators only work with black and white images
AI image generators convert images into text instead of text into images

exercises.explain: The real magic is bridging two different data worlds: discrete text tokens go in, and a brand-new continuous pixel grid comes out — created fresh, not copied from a search.
